review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-115-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: Non-alphabetical import/export ordering for the new catalog module
findings.evidence: index.js places `export * from "./api-fixture-ui-readiness-catalog.js"` after api-permission-audit-binding even though it sorts first; the .mjs script lists CP115 imports (PERMISSION_KERNEL_CP115_*) ahead of the CP114 imports.
findings.recommendation: Optional cosmetic reorder to keep imports/exports alphabetical. No functional impact; does not block closeout.

findings.title: Plan-pack unit match not verifiable from the staged diff
findings.evidence: requirePlanPackUnitMatch(cp115PlanPack, createPermissionKernelCp115CoveredUnitIds(), "CP00-115") depends on docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json, which is not in this diff. The 150 generated unit IDs (RP02.P03.M06.S07 … RP02.P04.M05.S07) are internally consistent, but their exact equality to the plan's CP00-115 entry can only be confirmed against the plan file at runtime.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the plan file's CP00-115 unit list matches the generated IDs; the validation script already enforces this when executed.

parsed_review.rationale: Unit coverage is exact: 65 API-terminal + 85 UI-readiness = 150 units, first=RP02.P03.M06.S07, last=RP02.P04.M05.S07, across 11 micro-phases (M06 starts at S07, contiguous with CP114's prior S06 terminal). I independently recomputed all eight deliverable buckets from the title/phase tables and every total matches the asserted values (implementation 48, contract 15, security_audit 12, test 12, hermes_evidence 6, claude_review 12, ui 42, fixture 3 = 150). CP114 fixture inheritance is real (matrix.api_fixture_matrix = createPermissionKernelCp114ApiSyntheticFixtureMatrix(), inherited_api_version permission_api_binding.v0.1, api_fixture_surface_count 4). UI readiness defines 13 states including denied (deny), review-required (review_required), and no_unauthorized_count_leak (redacted), plus permission_badge and audit_hint_display catalog surfaces. Leakage prevention holds: freezeUiState hardcodes unauthorized_count_exposed=false and hidden_field_names_exposed=false (overriding any per-state input), and validation loops re-assert these plus all write/share/AI/approval flags false; the full 15-flag no_write_attestation is shared by reference across every catalog row. The diff adds no runtime UI/API routes, persistence adapters, policy mutation, audit/product/database writes, idempotency/lock persistence, external share/export, AI retrieval, or LDIP — it only constructs frozen metadata, docs, tests, and read-only validation. HRX embedded boundary and handoff to CP00-116 / RP02.P04.M05.S08 are correct and consistently asserted in the catalog, contract JSON, tests, and validator. The two P3 items are cosmetic/scope notes and do not affect closeout.
